title: High-Throughput Beta-glucuronidase (GUS) Assay for Phaeodactylum tricornutum
author: Erin Garza
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bbexijfn
materials: To lyse the cells, add 150 µl bacterial protein extraction reagent (B-PER, ThermoFisher) to each well and mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer 250 μl of each P
steps.instruction: Transfer 250 μl of each P. tricornutum culture to a 96-well plate and centrifuge at 3000 x g for 10 min. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Centrifuge plate for 10 min at 3000 x g
steps.instruction: Centrifuge plate for 10 min at 3000 x g. Transfer supernatants to a new 96-well plate, being careful not to disturb the cell debris.

steps.title: Transfer 50 µl of each lysate...
steps.instruction: Transfer 50 µl of each lysate to a new plate and add 125 µl GUS extraction buffer + 1 mM MUG to each well. Incubate the plate for 1 h at 37°C. GUS extraction buffer= 50 mM NaPO 4 H 2 (pH 7), 0.1% Triton X-100, and 10 µM βME

steps.title: To stop the reaction, add 150...
steps.instruction: To stop the reaction, add 150 µl GUS stop buffer (0.2 M Na 2 CO 3 ) to each well and mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer 200 µl quenched reaction to an opaque 96-well plate
steps.instruction: Transfer 200 µl quenched reaction to an opaque 96-well plate.

steps.title: Determine fluorescence using a plate reader. Settings
steps.instruction: Determine fluorescence using a plate reader. Settings: excitation- 360 nm; emission- 440 nm.

steps.title: If fluorescence readings are too high...
steps.instruction: If fluorescence readings are too high to get a readout, dilute with additional stop buffer.

steps.title: Use remaining cell lysates to perform...
steps.instruction: Use remaining cell lysates to perform a BCA assay (or an equivalent assay) to normalize the GUS activity to total cell protein for each culture.
